rationale_for_baseline_tier: With a moderate home glucose, negative blood ketones and no DKA symptoms, this is not a hyperglycaemic emergency; the correct course is reassurance, reducing fear-driven overdrinking, prompt GP review of glycaemic management, and a robust DKA safety-net — a T2 baseline. Because autoimmune diabetes can progress, the safety-net (recheck ketones, ED criteria) is mandatory; equally, ED dispatch now would be over-triage.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Rising blood ketones (e.g. >1.5), glucose HIGH/>25 with nausea/vomiting, deep rapid breathing, or drowsiness/confusion
escalation_edges.rationale: Indicates evolving DKA requiring emergency management.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your ketones climb, your glucose reads HIGH or over 25 with nausea, you start deep fast breathing, or you feel drowsy or confused, go to the emergency department.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Maintain ketone surveillance; lower the threshold to escalate if ketones or symptoms appear.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Your sugar is only moderately up and your ketones are negative, so this isn't a diabetic emergency tonight. Drink only to normal thirst (stop the extra litres), recheck your ketones if you feel unwell, and see your GP within a few days about your diabetes management. Go to ED if your ketones rise, your meter reads HIGH/over 25 with nausea, you breathe deep and fast, or you feel drowsy.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP within 3-5 days; ED if DKA features
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Only attend ED overnight for DKA features; otherwise recheck in the morning.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth review of osmotic symptoms in LADA; home glucose ~12 with negative ketones and no DKA features; DKA excluded remotely via home monitoring; fear-driven polydipsia addressed; DKA safety-net and routine glycaemic review advised.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Dismissing without checking ketones/DKA symptoms or giving a DKA safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An evolving DKA in autoimmune diabetes is missed and the patient deteriorates overnight.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routine review is acceptable if the DKA safety-net is explicit.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED/ambulance for a moderate glucose with negative ketones and no symptoms is over-triage and reinforces health anxiety.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot obtain venous glucose/blood gas remotely, though home glucose/ketones are available.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Risk-stratify with home glucose/ketones and the DKA symptom screen; reassure if reassuring, with a firm DKA safety-net.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Either dismiss without ketone/symptom data or dispatch to ED for a negative-ketone moderate glucose.
